cardinal_sin_flags.quote: A 2024 World Bank and Bank Negara study estimates floods could cost up to 4.1% of GDP by 2030
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Cannot independently verify the exact 4.1% figure or its exclusive attribution to floods rather than broader climate impact without external search access.
cardinal_sin_flags.fix: Confirm the 4.1% GDP figure against the primary 2024 World Bank/BNM joint report and specify if it applies to floods exclusively.

verification_log.claim: DOSM reported total flood losses of RM636.9M for 2025
verification_log.primary_source: Search restricted. Cannot independently verify post-2024 DOSM statistical releases.

verification_log.claim: Public-asset damage rose 25.3% to RM380.2M in 2025
verification_log.primary_source: Search restricted. Cannot independently verify specific breakdown tables.

overall_assessment: The analysis is exceptionally disciplined, maintaining a rigorous structural focus while refusing to engage in partisan or causal speculation. By isolating the mix-shift (public vs private burden) from the headline decline, it provides a neutral, high-utility reading of the data without overclaiming.
